reason: field/object conflation: Q4874481 'Bayesian statistics' is P31 Q11862829 (academic discipline) and P31 Q17737 (branch of statistics) -- a whole subfield of statistics theory. ProbabilityTheory.posterior (Mathlib/Probability/Kernel/Posterior.lean:70, def posterior (κ : Kernel Ω 𝔏) (μ : Measure Ω) := ((μ ⊗ₘ κ).map Prod.swap).condKernel) is one narrow construction -- the posterior kernel of a prior/likelihood pair -- not a formalization of the discipline. note: legitimate related pairing: traj (Mathlib/Probability/Kernel/IonescuTulcea/Traj.lean:518) is the Ionescu-Tulcea construction composing a family κ n : Kernel (Π i : Iic n, X i) (X (n+1)) of Markov kernels into a limit kernel over infinite trajectories -- it generalizes discrete-time Markov chains (Q16109430) since κ n may depend on the full past not just the current state (no homogeneity/Markov-property restriction imposed), but it is the closest and directly relevant Mathlib construction; no more specific 'discrete-time Markov chain' decl exists (checked Mathlib/Probability/Kernel/: Irreducible.lean discusses Markov chains only in prose comments, no dedicated MarkovChain structure)
verify_note: traj at IonescuTulcea/Traj.lean:518; grep: no MarkovChain decl in Mathlib; base generalization label honest; ok

reason: concept mismatch: Q579515 'Rao–Blackwell theorem' is a distinct statistical-estimation theorem (about improving an estimator by conditioning on a sufficient statistic) that is related to the law of total variance (both involve conditional variance decompositions and are used together in estimator-variance arguments) but is not the same statement -- integral_condVar_add_variance_condExp does not formalize Rao–Blackwell's estimator-improvement claim.
